title: approvals — Artisan commands
description: Sixteen commands split into three families: introspection (list-*, describe-*), authoring (test-expression, test-selector, activate-*, deactivate-*), and operations (approve/reject/withdraw via CLI, sweepers, audit reports, remediation).

commands.purpose: List approval_instances filtered by status + tenant + action_key. --status accepts comma-list. Useful for support diagnostics + audit spot-checks.

Print full state of one instance: template snapshot, requirements + resolved_approvers, decision timeline, context_json (redacted per caller's ClI-audit tier). Includes delegation substitutions if any occurred.
List approval templates. Support-scoped. --active-only filters to is_active=true; default shows every version.
Flip is_active=true on a template row. Observer enforces the one-is-active invariant (deactivates the peer). Support-tooling — normal path is HTTP PATCH.
Flip is_active=false. Leaves the template row in place for audit.
List the approvable_actions registry. --namespace filters to one owning module. Confirms every #[AsApprovableAction] decorator was discovered at boot.

Dry-run a template's when_expression against a synthetic context. Prints parse tree + evaluation result (true|false). --context-json accepts inline JSON or a path to a fixture file. --action optional; when provided, the schema for the action's context_variables gets applied so the caller sees which vars are recognised.

Dry-run an approver selector expression against a specific tenant. Prints the resolved User set + delegation substitutions. Useful when template authors want to preview 'who would be paged' without submitting a real request.

CLI approve — support-tooling for the (rare) case where a customer's approver can't access the UI. Enforces the same caller_is_approver + status invariants as the HTTP endpoint. Records the CLI operator via `--as=platform_ops_user_id` when the approving user is impersonated for support purposes.
CLI reject. --reason required (min 10 chars — SOC 2 evidence).
Withdraw a pending instance. Default caller = the requester. --as-admin allows a platform_admin to cancel on the requester's behalf (rare — audited as an admin-override with reason required).
Force-run the sweeper (usually scheduled every 5 minutes). --dry-run prints the instances that WOULD transition without touching the DB.
Force-run the reminder sweeper. --dry-run prints the reminders that WOULD be sent.

Generate a compliance snapshot (SOC 2 CC6.3 + ISO 27001 A.9.2 evidence). --tenant accepts comma-list (empty = all tenants); --from/--to define the period (default: last 90 days). --format=json | csv. --out writes to a local file; omitting streams to stdout.

Find instances in the WEDGED state (status='approved' AND executed_at IS NULL AND updated_at older than 1 hour) — re-dispatch the underlying action via ApprovalInstanceExecutor. --instance targets one; without it iterates the wedged set. --dry-run prints without re-dispatch.

Force-run the nightly reconciler. --dry-run prints invariant violations without fixing. Fixes: (1) demote extra is_active=true peers to false; (2) log orphan approval_instances pointing to deleted templates; (3) re-evaluate delegation-touched requirements + dispatch supplementary reminders where delegates changed.
